info.description: The Selling Partner API for Vendor Direct Fulfillment Sandbox Test Data provides programmatic access to vendor direct fulfillment sandbox test data.
info.title: Selling Partner API for Vendor Direct Fulfillment Sandbox Test Data

post.description: Submits a request to generate test order data for Vendor Direct Fulfillment API entities.

parameters.name: body
parameters.description: The request payload containing parameters for generating test order data scenarios.

400.description: Request has missing or invalid parameters and cannot be parsed.

403.description: Indicates that access to the resource is forbidden. Possible reasons include Access Denied, Unauthorized, Expired Token, or Invalid Signature.

404.description: The resource specified does not exist.

413.description: The request size exceeded the maximum accepted size.

415.description: The request payload is in an unsupported format.

429.description: The frequency of requests was greater than allowed.

500.description: An unexpected condition occurred that prevented the server from fulfilling the request.

503.description: Temporary overloading or maintenance of the server.

OrderScenarioRequest.description: The party identifiers required to generate the test data.

sellingParty.description: The identifier of the selling party or vendor.

shipFromParty.description: The warehouse code of the vendor.

GenerateOrderScenarioRequest.description: The request body for the generateOrderScenarios operation.

orders.description: The list of test orders requested as indicated by party identifiers.

ErrorList.description: A list of error responses returned when a request is unsuccessful.

errors.description: An array of individual error objects containing error details.

code.description: An error code that identifies the type of error that occurred.

message.description: A message that describes the error condition.

details.description: Additional details that can help the caller understand or fix the issue.

Error.description: Error response returned when the request is unsuccessful.

TransactionReference.description: A GUID assigned by Amazon to identify this transaction.

transactionId.description: A GUID (Globally Unique Identifier) assigned by Amazon to uniquely identify the transaction.

PartyIdentification.description: The identification object for the party information. For example, warehouse code or vendor code. Please refer to specific party for more details.

partyId.description: Assigned identification for the party. For example, warehouse code or vendor code. Please refer to specific party for more details.
